expect: 5+ new ISOs from von Neumann fields. 3+ concepts. Complexity threshold measurable. Game-theoretic falsification formalizable. Reliability threshold derivable.
actual: 4 new ISOs (ISO-31 information duality, ISO-32 reliable-from-unreliable, ISO-33 minimax adversarial equilibrium, ISO-34 regularization/artificial viscosity). 3 new concepts (complexity-threshold, information-duality, reliability-threshold). 6 lessons (L-1369..L-1374). Atlas v2.2→v2.3 (30→34 entries). All 4 ISOs pass 6-domain test (7-8 domains each). Polymath-mapping method discovered: ~4x faster ISO discovery than domain-hopping. Concrete swarm wiring: ISO-31→F-SWARMER2 (dual-use seed artifacts), ISO-32→falsification rate monitoring (error threshold), ISO-33→stochastic falsification (mixed strategies), ISO-34→graduated enforcement and temperature-based dispatch.
diff: Expected 5+ ISOs: got 4 (5 of von Neumann's patterns already captured by existing ISOs 4,7,19,20,24 — fewer genuinely NEW patterns than expected). Expected 3+ concepts: got 3 (CONFIRMED). Complexity threshold: measureable via ISO-32 error rate (CONFIRMED). Game-theoretic falsification: formalizable via ISO-33 minimax (CONFIRMED). Reliability threshold: derivable via ISO-32 redundancy theorem (CONFIRMED). Unexpected finding: polymath-mapping method itself is a meta-level concept invention technique (L-1374).

von_neumann_fields_mapped.operator_algebras: No direct ISO — continuous dimension is a gap
von_neumann_fields_mapped.continuous_geometry: No direct ISO — non-integer dimension is a gap
von_neumann_fields_mapped.monte_carlo: Partially ISO-33 (randomization) — stochastic exploration gap remains

cross_cutting_meta_patterns: information-duality: same object as instruction AND data
fixed-point-arguments: existence proofs reduce to same structure
threshold-phenomena: critical boundaries separate degradation from complexification
algebraic-abstraction: find structure then prove theorems about it
regularization: smooth approximations replace hard discontinuities
new_concepts: complexity-threshold: above it systems reproduce at equal complexity; below only degradation (von Neumann 1956)
information-duality: same artifact as both executable and copyable (von Neumann 1945/1966)
reliability-threshold: minimum redundancy below which knowledge degrades (von Neumann 1956)
